evals.prompt: Analyze the latest UA run. Check for errors and give me a quick health report.
evals.expected_output: Logfire MCP arbitrary_query is called with a recent-runs discovery query. The main trace is identified. A health check query runs against the run_id. A health verdict (green/yellow/red) is produced with exception count, total spans, duration, and tool call summary.
expectations: The arbitrary_query tool (Logfire MCP) is called at least once
The discovery query uses a time-bounded filter (age parameter set, e.g. 1440 minutes)
A run_id or trace_id is identified from the discovery query before further analysis
The health check span summary query is executed (span_name, count, avg_dur_sec, has_exceptions)
A health verdict (green/yellow/red or similar) is stated
The report is written to {workspace}/work_products/logfire-eval/logfire_evaluation.md or the agent explains why it cannot resolve a workspace

evals.prompt: What happened in that heartbeat last night? I want to know what it did and if anything went wrong.
evals.expected_output: The heartbeat_significant query runs for the last 48 hours. The most recent heartbeat is selected and drilled into via its trace_id. A summary of what the heartbeat did (tools, wake_reason, duration) is provided.
expectations: The arbitrary_query tool is called with the heartbeat_significant query
The age parameter is at least 2880 (48 hours)
A specific heartbeat trace_id is selected for deep-dive
A second query uses the heartbeat trace_id to get the span breakdown
Exceptions within the heartbeat trace are noted if present

evals.prompt: The last run got cancelled unexpectedly mid-task. Can you analyze what happened and why it stopped?
evals.expected_output: The run is found via discovery query. The lifecycle exception query checks for execution_cancelled, budget_exceeded, and execution_error spans. The exception deep-dive query also runs. The analysis distinguishes whether the stop was user-initiated (execution_cancelled) or budget-hit (budget_exceeded) or a crash (execution_error).
expectations: A run discovery query is executed to find the relevant run
The span-level exception query is executed (is_exception = true)
A lifecycle span check is performed for budget_exceeded / execution_cancelled / execution_error
The analysis correctly identifies the stopping condition (cancelled vs budget vs error)
Tool execution summary is included (what tools were called before the stop)
The report includes a Recommendations section if the stop was unexpected
